column1: # apps/registry.tsv — the authoritative list of demo apps. ONE ROW PER APP.
# Everything that loops over apps reads this file (scripts/lib/apps.sh): the Gitea seeding, the
# Tekton pipeline/trigger rendering, the ArgoCD Applications, the ingress routes and `make verify`.
# Adding an app is a ROW plus (only if it is a new language) one `case` branch in lib/apps.sh.
# Why a file and not .env.example: this is STRUCTURE (which apps exist), not a tunable. Same shape
# as images/images.txt — an inventory the code iterates.
# The ingress HOSTNAME is DERIVED, not listed: app_host() (lib/apps.sh) returns <name>.${APP_DOMAIN},
# where APP_DOMAIN is ONE global in .env.example. There used to be a 5th column naming a per-app
# `<APP>_HOST` variable — which meant adding an app was a row here PLUS a line in .env.example, and
# no gate could see the second edit. Deriving it is what makes "ONE ROW" literally true (and it is
# also what lets a shared Istio Gateway admit every app at once with a single `*.${APP_DOMAIN}`).
# COLUMNS (tab-separated):
#   name      the app + its k8s namespace + its Harbor repo + its Gitea repos (<name>-app/-deploy)
#             + its ingress host (<name>.${APP_DOMAIN})
#   lang      java | go — selects the Tekton test task and the marker-rewrite rule (lib/apps.sh)
#   src       source dir; this dir IS the content of the <name>-app Gitea repo
#   deploy    kustomize dir; this dir IS the content of the <name>-deploy Gitea repo
# name
javawebapp
gowebapp
